column35: Comments
Cool Scheme! If it comes down to a turn or two for elimination, its 100% arnim zolas fault for stepping in when I could have beaten the other 2 in the 1/3 shuffle.

Out of Time was broken! We managed to fight Beyonder 3 times in a turn (including 1 for "free" with cosmic treat). Phyla-Vell did not do much, but was ramping up just fine with Nebula. Probably would have been good if game had lasted for 30+ turns.

Luck is very important in this game. All but one masterstrike came out on Magus. This gave me 24 vp that other players might not have had. A bagel shuffle with scheme twists also game me several turns at the start of the game to just recruit with a worry of the city. Game went longer than I hoped, but hopefully I did enough. If not congratulations to the top 4. I am still rooting for JParnell.

Oh whoops oh, has the melk spoilt?

For my first attempt I tried to select for boosting Nebula with Instinct and Tech, selecting Bruce Banner and Amadeus Cho for that and their strongish cards. I ended up not able to buy the uncommons from Amadeus Cho that would have been amazing, but had a tense game with them that took 36 turns nonetheless. I wanted to try to get a better second attempt and although especially Bruce Banner's 2-cost made for some pretty consistent 7-8 sized hands for deck 2 I decided that I needed to rescue bystanders if I really wanted to improve my score. So for attempt 2 I selected Hiroim and Steve Rogers, Director of S.H.I.E.L.D. Partly also to help with KO-ing starters, which took too long in attempt 1. I ended up losing attempt 2, so then it obviously wasn't going to be better than attempt 1. Twist 9 was drawn in turn 29 or so. Attempt 2 was working fine otherwise, just needed to get some steam for finishing the Masterminds, but if the last twist comes, you can't escape that.

Nebula's 3-cost was pretty good for KO-ing starters in both games, but in Attempt 1 I managed to KO plenty of starters with her. It did however take too long. Deck 1 was mostly Nebula focused, ended up getting all 5 of her 5-costs and her rare, but only 1 of her 3-costs, which is why it took so long to KO the starters. Deck 2 was mostly Bruce Banner and Amadeus Cho (his 2-cost never showed unfortunately). I think it was a tough setup, so I'm not ashamed of taking 36 turns, but it isn't very fast either. The potential of extra VP from rescuing bystanders might just be the difference between getting into the top 4 or not. But perhaps the others have also chosen not to select those kinds of heroes. Taking so long did assist me in defeating almost all villains in the deck, which might help me get some more VP and a higher average PPT. If you were faster though, PPT would possibly be higher still, as more turns had happened and Mastermind hits are worth more than most cards in the Villain Deck.

In the beginning of especially game 1 there was some tension about preventing escapes. Some strong enemies came out which I couldn't reduce with their cosmic Threat easily (because those colors were either completely absent [Ranged]or not yet in HQ and decks [Instinct]). it worked out fine, but had me make some tough choices (for example defeating Primus when it wasn't an ideal time to do so by discarding a Nebula 3-cost that would have provided other strong benefits). Also a few wounds could not be prevented and made the start slightly less ideal than I wanted. The Shapers of Worlds were useful in expanding the recruiting options and giving a free somewhat expensive Hero to whichever deck benefited most. The absence of any Ranged cards did however make them as tough as some of the masterminds! The Twists moving around the Mastermind from being the active one and lurking was pretty fun to manage, although it did mean that some of them were only there for 1 turn and then gone again (due to back to back Twists). The breathing room a twist provided was nice and sometimes necessary to get enough attack and manage the City. In Attempt 2 this was too extreme, as there was often nothing to defeat and the last tiwst came  way too early.

Pretty high fun level. The Epicness of Malekith and Magus didn't mean that much, because in my game it was easy to counter their extra strength by simply defeating the Villains in the City. Malekith also lacked targets for Villainous Weapons. Getting only 2 tactics made especially Malekith much weaker, but all of them actually, because the Master Strikes porbably won't help the same ones. I ended up getting lucky with Master Strikes, getting 1 for The Beyonder and 1 for Magus (which added 6 VP to the game). The other 3 I didn't get to see. Arnim Zola ended up being about as strong and stronger than the Beyonder was with his with Cosmic Threat on the turns he got defeated. I can't remember playing this Scheme before, but it is now one of my favourites, because of the potential for more points! 8 tactics is obviously worth more than 4, no matter which Masterminds you pick. (Although one 7 pointer versus 4 5-pointers adds only 10 points with Final Blow Rules). So, even though the Masterminds are slightly weakened by sharing the Master Strike pool and only having 2 tactics each, it can get really hard I think. I can easily imagine that it gets extremely difficult to win without using brokenly strong Heroes if you use four Epic 7 pointers as Masterminds.

I knew my first game was lost early on but finished it to scout the second game. I swapped in Yondu for Cap'41 to manage the top of the deck, which he did an amazing job of. No Pocket Dimensions, no escapes, no wounds(!). My P2 deck had cleared all of its starter cards, and I was cruising towards victory. But then starting with turn 29, I had 5 Twists in a row. It was worse because due to Danger Sense I knew they were coming, but couldn't do anything about them. On my P1 deck's last turn, I didn't draw a Yondu card to bury the final twist deeper. One of the most disappointing ends to a game, and a very disappointing end to my tournament.
You guys and your Wolfsbane shenanigans...

I decided to give one play supporting Wolfsbane, and one supporting Ultimate Spider-Man.  It wasn't even close, I don't know why I bothered with anything other than a Spider deck.

I didn't see any Save the Day until turn 16.  On turn 22, I'd enough of them that generated enough attack to clear the City (including an 18 attack Galactus), get Annihilus' last Tactic, and deliver the final blow.

What a difference a rare makes. The first playthrough did not go well. Winning just as the villain deck was about to empty with multiple escapes. The second game Mirage's rare turned up early and generated so much attack supported by that hand regularly having 9 cards due to Empathic Links.
The other side was consistent but Ultimate Spider-mans rare consistently hiding hurt it. Silk carrying that side along with many sidekicks.

Very difficulty. With both Weaponized Galactus and Demon Bear returning multiple times this was  very difficult. We were able to have a hand of spider-people doing the 2 cost thing that allowed us to get a victory but it was a slug fest.

Game went about as smoothly as I could have hoped. Managed to avoid the MS effect most of the time and used the Phalanx to help thin decks down. One player went heavy into SHIELD Level and the other Tech. One player did very well and the other.... well, the numbers speak for themselves.
It took us a while to build our decks up, but we eventually got there and starting smacking Mephisto when we could. Sai Blades was hitting hard with Deathlok and Punisher feeding the 2 cost ramp.

Both my games were pretty long, I couldn't really trigger the best combos to use in these games. I wanted to use a Marvel Kinght and a S.H.I.E.L.D. hero, but didn't really have the best options since I already used them in previous games. I expect to be dead last because of my high turn count, and also because not using Mockingbird :D But the setup itself was great, not too complicated but not too easy, so I had a great time =) Thanks for the whole tournament!

First game Punisher and Moon Knight carried the table to the point that the right hand didn't get to do anything.

Game 2 was a bit more even across where Elektra's uncommon got to shine with her first strike to get damage early on Mephisto. 

Game 2 was two turns slower but .27 more vp points per turn so putting that in as a test to see for the next tournament what I should focus on more since I had a blast this first time around.
Loading up on Marvel Knights helped block a bunch of wounds and kept my game from going off the rails. Took a while to put some punch in my line-up.

The first win took almost 40 turns, so I was glad a second time worked out better. Both were very close to losing, I liked how this put the pressure on, but things like Colossus and relatively weak villains and Nimrod made it possible to hold on until I got things going. Lady Thor helped since so much was focused on building recruit, and Apocalyptic Kitty Pride helped set up the HQ so it was mostly tech heroes to empower Jocasta.

column35: Cool synergy with Ultron villains teaming up with Nimrod, it felt comic lore possible. I liked the X-Men "villains" due to their beneficial effects until Wolverine escaped and gave me game long wounds. I liked the balance of being empowered by tech while also having to worry about tech empowering Ulton villains. The Fragmented Reality scheme was a little frustrating at first since only two city spaces exist and allow for lots of escapes but that only really happened the first few turns and then once again near the end. Definitely a fun playthrough!

Draw power go BRRRRRR. A lot of villains seemed to have had weird interactions with the city being missing. We also struggled on which game to choose. They were very close, but we ultimately went with the fewer rounds at the cost of VP/turn.

I think it has been a long time since I dropped that many f-bombs in a Legendary game.  I messed up my first game by forgetting that I can avert Nimrod's Strike by flashing a Tech card, but still lost on turn 25 with very little in the Victory Piles.  The second game somehow felt even harder, but Mr. Fantastic's rare pulled me through by the skin of my teeth.  I feel brutalized, but I have to admit it was thrilling.
First game was rough.. ended the second attempt as fast as I could 😰

I took Invisible Woman to give me something with KO power and ended up not getting her KO card in the HQ until the end of the game. Thing ended up doing exactly what I wanted him to do, though, converting my recruit into attack so I could whack the mastermind. Got really lucky with the first 8 cards containing two bystanders and two master strikes; gave me enough time to recruit some stuff before the escapes started hitting. Lost the first game HARD.

First game I lost on turn 18. I had so much recruit and no attack. None. I couldn’t stop anyone except the Thor corps. It went very poorly.
Second game felt similar at first but somehow I hung on through the twists and kraven to only have 9 KOs when I eked out the W. I’m glad I switched out lady Thor. I thought she would pair well with captain marvel and the Thor corps. But with just one city space for each player there was no room for all that recruit. IW really helped provide more attack and the HD was much friendlier providing more attack heroes. Thank goodness this is over!

Initial idea was: getting more chrome helmets! Meaning Ant-man and Lady Thor, the former for Jocasta synergies and against manster strike, Lady Thor for the Thor Corps synergy. 
Then somewhy I thought too much and went for Captain Marvel insted of Ant Man for even more green/blue synergies. 

First game I lost on turn 15. 
Second game I had like 8 or 9 KOd heroes by that time (thanks Kraven for extra KO and early Wolverine for nice wounds..). The first thor corps appeared in Turn 15, so not much combo potential from them..   Then they arrived in great numbers, but I was merely waiting for the scheme twists to appear and end my suffering. But they did not! Living on the edge, I could deliver two blows to Nimrod, then have a round with 15+ attack but not enough recruit, then finally had a combo of 21+ attack with enough recruit as well to get two tactics and a final blow. 

Still, this is a hateful scheme :):) The hero setup vs Nimrod with different scheme probably would have been fun.

Is there a 0/5 fun factor? I think I had more fun packing up than playing. First game I pulled 4/2 on both hands, so that’s an automatic 4/10 escapes without any counter play. I didn’t defeat a villain until the 14th turn!
Second game was better but it’s just not possible to get cards in your deck fast enough. Maybe I should have switched to spiders instead.

I had to be playing this wrong. Only one villain per player out and a guaranteed escape for every villain card played unless you can get some attack in your hand fast? Ugh. If I see this scheme again I’ll just report a loss and save the time setting up.
Why!? Why!?

What an intense game. After a good but losing first attempt, made some strategic choices to maximize the chance to win, and pulled it out! “Comic” side was a mostly blue/green deck with lots of good cards (2 rares, multiple uncommons). “MCU” side was even better. In both games our Black Widow deck never really got going and the Ice Man deck took off getting Bishops Rare (both games!) early. We were able to manage the Angle cards in the city with few KOed and Apocalypse only hitting one hero in his tactics.

what a difference in games,
game 1 went straight to 0, when i finally had some combo/synergies is lost hard
game 2 just finished and i had some amazingly explosive turns, black widow popping hard, having jean grey's rare played at the turn i would empty out the bystander deck into my vp pile, wouldve ended quicker if i wasnt as unlucky on revealing apocalypses 'shuffle back' tactic 3 times in a row... 
focused on black widow/red-black on left side
focused on ice man-cyclops blue/green right side.
it felt amazing hitting huge numbers and combo's later in the game.
angel's cards absolutely helped as well and weren't a lost inclusion for either piles.
game 1 wouldve been a 1/5, game 2 def a 5/5 with maybe a 3/5 for early setup stage of the game so i settled on 3/5 overall.

column35: This was an excellent game. I had a lot of fun. Was touch and go for a little while early on but pulled out the win in the end!

We had a great game, the heroes were strong enough to battle a hard mm and scheme combination. We won the first game but the second after a really bad set of cards from the villain deck we lost in 16 turns. So a game that could be really fun but too dependable of luck.

I was sooo close! I played one more turn after recording my loss details, and I easily finished off Apocalypse for the consolation "win". While I appreciate the overlapping synergies which layered up the difficulty, it proved to be slightly too much. Four Horsemen are already tough to beat with Apoc, but then they were further boosted by captured Angels. (I did appreciate the choice of Angel being the "captured" hero in the villain deck.) Phalanx was a challenging choice as they were helpful for a while to thin out the deck, but made for tension when couldn't fight them later in the game as they would KO a non-grey hero, getting closer to the Scheme's Evil Wins. Had been a while since I faced off against Apocalypse, and was reminded how rough he can be. The draws from the Villain Deck can be very swingy. MS's early are no big deal as decks are mostly grey heroes, but they're rough late. ST's feel so good to get lucky and hit back-to-back while no Angels were captured, but can also hit at mean times otherwise. I wish I had given a bit more thought to fighting Apocalypse at the end of the game. I knew which two Tactics he had left (bystander/shuffle and KO heroes from discard). I simply punched him when I had the chance, but if I had thought about it more, I should have waited until the other player had 0 non-grey heroes in their discard (if possible). Instead, that was the Tactic I hit while already having 8 non-grey heroes KO'ed, and it lost me the game! The tough setup felt mostly fun, but perhaps just a little too tough to call it enjoyable, even if I could appreciate how it was attacking non-grey heroes from several different angles. I'll get them next week!

column35:
With all the Demolishing going on, and several of the pre-assigned Heroes offering card draw, I thought just leaning in to card draw would be a straightforward way to go. I picked Ant-Man and No-Name as my additional Heroes, thinking that No-Name's Surprise Attack would pair well with Wasp's commons that needed a Covert trigger; while Ant-Man's Tech cards would help out Hulkbuster Iron-Man's Pound for Pound.

The No-Name/Wasp pair actually worked pretty well, but Pound for Pound never showed up so the number of Tech cards in play was a little out of balance. I also didn't do quite a good enough job focusing the two decks on just No-Name/Wasp or Hulkbuster/Ant-Man, which led to a couple wasted turns where Chivalrous Duel was in effect and I couldn't muster enough attack from a single Hero to do much of anything.

Still, on the whole, both decks got off to a pretty quick start, especially with No-Name Feasting through player 2's starter cards. There were a few stalled turns in the middle, so it still ended up being a relatively slow game, but I felt like I had good control most of the time. On the whole, heavy card draw is just a widely-useful approach to almost any setup.
Chivalrous Duel was not much of an issue, since most heroes were relatively capable of bringing high attack power. Unfortunately, Wasp was mostly useless.
